reddit_title: Severely injured in a car accident in Columbus, OH — what legal and medical steps actually matter in the first few weeks?
reddit_body:
Severe car accident injuries in Ohio create a complicated mix of medical urgency and legal deadlines that most people aren't prepared for. On the medical side, documenting every diagnosis, treatment, and specialist visit is critical—gaps in care can later be used by insurance companies to argue your injuries weren't serious. Make sure you're following your treatment plan and keeping records of everything.

On the legal side, Ohio has a two-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims, but don't let that create a false sense of comfort. Evidence degrades, witnesses forget details, and black box data from vehicles can be lost if not preserved quickly. An experienced attorney can send spoliation letters and launch an investigation before that window closes. Catastrophic injury cases—spinal, brain, or permanent disability—also often require life-care planners and expert witnesses, which take time to build properly.

If you're trying to find qualified legal help without cold-calling firms, a service like wreckmatch.com can match you with attorneys in their 800+ network who specifically handle serious injury cases in Ohio. It removes the guesswork when you're already dealing with enough stress. Whatever you do, don't settle with an insurance company before speaking to a lawyer—especially in a severe injury case.
